master_row.title: Spectral Density Index (TZP-Weighted)
master_row.canonical_statement: The spectral density index is defined by $\eta(\omega)=\omega^{-\beta}e^{-\omega/\omega_0}$, where $\omega_0$ is a characteristic frequency fixed by $u_T$ and $\beta$ is a dimensionless exponent determined by the hyper-dimensional span.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as an exponential / gaussian profile relating ω, β, u_T, S_.
master_row.dictionary: The spectral density index (()) is defined as (()=^-,e^-/_0), where (_0) is a characteristic frequency determined by (u_T) and () is a dimensionless exponent that depends on the hyper‑dimensional span.
master_row.encyclopedia: $()$ weights frequency contributions by combining power-law scaling with exponential cutoff suppression. Interpretive role: This index controls how singular corrections reweight spectral content across scales. It becomes a practical input for noise models, fluctuation spectra, and any later renormalization flow that depends on frequency distribution.
